comment.body:
rough vol says that vol has power-law decaying correlations. but pure rough models are non-Markovian and computationally heavy... so Guyon proposes a 4-factor Markovian approximation reproducing “rough-like” vol paths. 
but 2-3-factor Markovians are enough; like two-factor Bergomi or multi-factor Heston. you can still get the power-law behavior, but without full fractional calculus. 
a 2-factor Bergomi can outperform a pure rough vol model when you do SPX smirk fitting. the SPX ATM skew vs maturity does not necessary strictly follow a pure power-law. it can increase less steeply for very short expiries and flattens for long expiries, which a simple rough model would miss
comment.raw_body:
rough vol says that vol has power-law decaying correlations. but pure rough models are non-Markovian and computationally heavy... so Guyon proposes a 4-factor Markovian approximation reproducing “rough-like” vol paths. 
but 2-3-factor Markovians are enough; like two-factor Bergomi or multi-factor Heston. you can still get the power-law behavior, but without full fractional calculus. 
a 2-factor Bergomi can outperform a pure rough vol model when you do SPX smirk fitting. the SPX ATM skew vs maturity does not necessary strictly follow a pure power-law. it can increase less steeply for very short expiries and flattens for long expiries, which a simple rough model would miss
